task_updates.executor_notes: Verified the payment cog startup and worker path with `tests/test_scheduler.py`: pending confirmations are re-registered through `bot.add_view(...)`, inflight recovery runs before the worker starts, claimed rows execute through the atomic claim path, terminal results notify the configured destination, and producer callbacks are handed off via direct `bot.get_cog()` lookup. Fixed startup readiness sequencing and made token-label rendering tolerate service doubles without provider metadata.

Verified the admin payment surfaces with `tests/test_social_route_tools.py`: payment-route CRUD, redacted wallet/payment inspection, and retry/hold/release/cancel controls are reachable; `payment_requests`, `payment_channel_routes`, and `wallet_registry` remain excluded from `QUERYABLE_TABLES`; and the admin agent prompt/catalog already exposes the dedicated payment tools. Added db-handler-aware guild resolution for the dedicated payment tools and a compatibility shim so wallet listing works against both the production handler and lightweight test doubles without exposing full wallet addresses.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `PaymentCog` now follows the queue-worker pattern with atomic `claim_due_payment_requests(...)`, re-registers persistent `PaymentConfirmView` instances on startup via `bot.add_view(...)`, emits terminal result notifications, and hands producer outcomes back through direct `bot.get_cog()` dispatch. Verified in `tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Yes. The admin surfaces expose payment-route CRUD plus redacted wallet/payment query and control tools through the admin agent, while `payment_requests`, `payment_channel_routes`, and `wallet_registry` stay off `QUERYABLE_TABLES`. Verified in `tests/test_social_route_tools.py`.
